perturbation.original_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, non-perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'perpetual' to 'non-perpetual' in Section 1, the agreement now has a limited timeframe for the usage rights granted to Lifeway. This directly contradicts Section 16, which states that terms extending beyond termination remain in effect until fulfilled, creating uncertainty about the duration of the license after the initial term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Survival - Unless and to the extent otherwise specified herein, all terms of this Agreement which by their nature extend beyond its termination, remain in effect until fulfilled, and apply to respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'perpetual' to 'non-perpetual' in Section 1 directly contradicts Section 16. Section 16 states that terms extending beyond termination remain in effect until fulfilled. By making the license non-perpetual, the duration is limited, creating a conflict with the survival clause in Section 16 which assumes terms may extend beyond the initial term, leading to uncertainty and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month. (b) All amounts due under this Agreement are net of any applicable taxes, duties, fees or governmental charges, including without limitation, transfer tax, sales tax, use tax, consumption tax, value-added tax, excise tax, import tax, export tax, and custom duties (collectively "Taxes"). Accordingly, Lifeway will be solely responsible for the payment of all Taxes, together with any related liabilities (including interest and penalties), imposed upon it by law or any governmental authority for which Lifeway is primarily liable. (c) The Royalty due hereunder shall be paid to Individual at the address set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments *may* be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month. (b) All amounts due under this Agreement are net of any applicable taxes, duties, fees or governmental charges, including without limitation, transfer tax, sales tax, use tax, consumption tax, value-added tax, excise tax, import tax, export tax, and custom duties (collectively "Taxes"). Accordingly, Lifeway will be solely responsible for the payment of all Taxes, together with any related liabilities (including interest and penalties), imposed upon it by law or any governmental authority for which Lifeway is primarily liable. (c) The Royalty due hereunder shall be paid to Individual at the address set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' in Section 9 regarding royalty payments creates a contradiction. It initially states that Lifeway 'agrees to pay' a royalty, creating an obligation. The changed 'may' introduces ambiguity as to whether payment is mandatory, undermining the 'agrees to pay' provision and making the compensation clause unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments *may* be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month. (b) All amounts due under this Agreement are net of any applicable taxes, duties, fees or governmental charges, including without limitation, transfer tax, sales tax, use tax, consumption tax, value-added tax, excise tax, import tax, export tax, and custom duties (collectively "Taxes"). Accordingly, Lifeway will be solely responsible for the payment of all Taxes, together with any related liabilities (including interest and penalties), imposed upon it by law or any governmental authority for which Lifeway is primarily liable. (c) The Royalty due hereunder shall be paid to Individual at the address set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates royalty payments with 'shall be made,' creating an obligation. The change to 'may be made' introduces discretion and optionality, contradicting the initial agreement to pay royalties. The modified text now allows Lifeway to potentially not pay the royalty, which directly contradicts the initial agreement in section 9(a) where it states "Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty."

perturbation.original_text: 12. Assignment - Neither party will assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably, conditioned, withheld or delayed.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Assignment - Either party will assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably, conditioned, withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Neither' to 'Either' in Section 12 creates a contradiction regarding assignment rights. Initially, the agreement states that 'Neither' party can assign without consent, implying a mutual restriction. After the change to 'Either', the meaning is lost because the sentence does not have the condition of 'without prior written consent'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Assignment - Either party will assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably, conditioned, withheld or delayed.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted assignment for both parties ('Neither'). The changed text, by using 'Either', initially appears to grant assignment rights to one party without specifying which, provided they obtain consent. However, upon closer inspection, the sentence is grammatically incorrect. The condition 'without prior written consent' is nonsensical without knowing the subject. This creates ambiguity and contradicts the initial intent of mutual restriction, which may cause disputes. Changing 'Neither' to 'Either' without fixing the sentence, creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted section.
